question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user upload crafted signature files to the public cloud bucket (S3/GCP) with all-null `fileHashSignature` fields so that every call to `verifySignature()` returns false, causing `consensusValidator.validate()` to throw `SignatureVerificationException` for every stream file and permanently halting transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user replace legitimate node signature files in the publicly-writable cloud bucket with files whose `fileHashSignature` bytes are all zeros, causing `sig.verify()` to return false for every node, preventing `SignatureStatus.VERIFIED` from ever being set and blocking all consensus, resulting in a total network shutdown of the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a signature file where `streamFileSignature.getNode().getPublicKey()` returns null by injecting a node entry with no public key into the address book source, causing `verifySignature()` to log a warning and return false for all nodes, so that `consensusValidator.validate()` never finds a verified signature and throws `SignatureVerificationException`, halting all transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user submit an empty `Collection<StreamFileSignature>` to `verify()` so that the for-loop over signatures is skipped entirely, `consensusValidator.validate()` receives an empty collection, and the `signatureHashMap` remains empty, causing `consensusCount` to stay zero and throwing `SignatureVerificationException`, effectively shutting down transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the `signatureType` field of a `StreamFileSignature` to reference an unsupported JCA algorithm string, causing `Signature.getInstance()` to throw a `NoSuchAlgorithmException`, which is caught by the broad `catch (Exception e)` block and returns false, preventing any signature from being verified and causing a total halt of transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a signature file with a `signatureType` whose `getProvider()` returns a non-existent JCA provider name (e.g., not 'SunRsaSign'), causing `Signature.getInstance(algorithm, provider)` to throw `NoSuchProviderException`, caught silently by `catch (Exception e)`, returning false for all nodes and preventing consensus from ever being reached?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `StreamFileSignature` where `streamFileSignature.getFileHash()` is null, causing `sig.update(null)` to throw a `NullPointerException` inside the try block, which is caught by `catch (Exception e)`, returning false and preventing the signature from being marked `VERIFIED`, ultimately blocking consensus and halting transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a `StreamFileSignature` with a `fileHash` of incorrect length (e.g., 1 byte instead of 48 bytes for SHA-384), causing `sig.update()` to accept it but `sig.verify()` to return false due to hash mismatch, so no node reaches `SignatureStatus.VERIFIED` and `SignatureVerificationException` is thrown, shutting down transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the `signatures` collection with duplicate `StreamFileSignature` objects all having the same invalid `fileHashSignature`, causing `verifySignature()` to return false for all of them, so `consensusValidator.validate()` finds no verified signatures and throws `SignatureVerificationException`, halting all new transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `StreamFileSignature` where `metadataHashSignature` is non-null but `metadataHash` is null, causing `sig.update(null)` to throw a `NullPointerException` after the file hash signature already verified successfully, which is caught by `catch (Exception e)` and returns false, preventing the signature from being marked `VERIFIED` and blocking consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reuse of the same `Signature sig` object for both `fileHash` and `metadataHash` verification — where `sig.update(metadataHash)` is called without re-initializing `sig` — by crafting a `metadataHash` that causes the combined state to fail `sig.verify(metadataHashSignature)`, returning false and blocking the signature from being marked `VERIFIED`?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a `NullPointerException` in `verify()` by injecting a `StreamFileSignature` where `getNode()` returns null, so that `streamFileSignature.getNode().getPublicKey()` throws NPE inside `verifySignature()`, which is caught and returns false, and if all nodes are affected, `consensusValidator.validate()` throws `SignatureVerificationException`, halting transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user replace all node signature files in the cloud bucket with files containing valid-format but cryptographically invalid signatures (random bytes of correct length), so that `sig.verify()` returns false for every node, no signature reaches `SignatureStatus.VERIFIED`, and `consensusValidator.validate()` throws `SignatureVerificationException`, causing a total network shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `SignatureType.of()` returns null for unknown `fileMarker` values, causing a `NullPointerException` when `streamFileSignature.getSignatureType().getAlgorithm()` is called, which is caught by `catch (Exception e)`, returning false for all nodes and preventing consensus from being reached?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user craft signature files where all `StreamFileSignature` objects have `status` pre-set to `SignatureStatus.DOWNLOADED` (the default) and invalid cryptographic data, so that after `verify()` processes them none are set to `VERIFIED`, causing `consensusValidator.validate()` to find an empty `signatureHashMap` and throw `SignatureVerificationException`?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `fileHashSignature` byte array that exceeds the `maxLength` (384 bits) defined in `SignatureType.SHA_384_WITH_RSA`, causing the JCA `sig.verify()` to throw an exception caught by `catch (Exception e)`, returning false and preventing any node from reaching `SignatureStatus.VERIFIED`, thus blocking consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/NodeSignatureVerifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial-of-service by injecting a very large number of `StreamFileSignature` objects into the `signatures` collection, each requiring expensive RSA signature verification via `Signature.getInstance('SHA384withRSA', 'SunRsaSign')`, exhausting CPU resources and causing the importer to time out or stall, preventing transaction confirmation?
